gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.description: Here you're going to see the motivation to build beyou, how to run all projects locally, how to collaborate with the app and other stuffs.

whyBeyou.title: Why Beyou
whyBeyou.paragraph: I've always had trouble keeping my tasks organized. I also wanted to see if I was progressing in certain areas of my life. For every app I tested, only one of those things was there. So I started building my own personal habit and routine organization app, more to learn programming, but also to ultimately have the product I always wanted, where I can easily create habits and tasks with life categories and see the evolution over time, not to mention the ease of creating customized daily routines and goals to always stay on track.

local.title: Local environment
local.description: Update preferences and keys in Settings.

localExecution.title: How to execute the project locally
localExecution.description: Follow this step‑by‑step guide to set up Beyou on your machine.

createFolder.title: Create a dedicated folder
createFolder.description: Create a folder where all projects will stay, like a 'beyou' folder.

installDependencies.title: Install dependencies
installDependencies.description: Go to each project folder and execute:

cloneDevEnv.title: Clone the development environment orchestrator
cloneDevEnv.description: Go back to the main folder and clone the dev‑env repository:

createEnvFile.title: Create the .env file
createEnvFile.description: Copy the content of .env.example inside a new .env file and fill your own values. At the end of this topic you will find a detailed explanation of each variable.

runScripts.title: Run the orchestration scripts
runScripts.description: You can choose between development mode (hot‑reload) or production‑like mode (optimized). Use the provided scripts:

scriptsExplanation.title: What each script does
scriptsExplanation.upDev: up‑dev.sh – starts the stack in development mode with hot‑reload for backend and frontend.
scriptsExplanation.upProd: up‑prod.sh – starts the stack in production‑like mode (builds the images and serves frontend via Nginx).
scriptsExplanation.down: down.sh – stops the stack (specify 'dev' or 'prod' mode).
scriptsExplanation.resetDb: reset‑db.sh – removes the PostgreSQL volume to reset the database.

envExplanation.title: Environment variables (.env)
envExplanation.description: Below are the required variables and how to fill them:
envExplanation.postgresDb: POSTGRES_DB – name of the database (default: beyou).
envExplanation.postgresPort: POSTGRES_PORT – port where PostgreSQL will be exposed (default: 5490).
envExplanation.tokenSecret: TOKEN_SECRET – a secret string used to sign JWT tokens; change it to a strong random value.
envExplanation.googleClientId: GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID – if you want Google OAuth, provide your OAuth 2.0 client ID.
envExplanation.cookieSecure: COOKIE_SECURE – set to 'true' for HTTPS, 'false' for local development.
envExplanation.mailSettings: Mail settings (optional) – fill if you want email functionality.
envExplanation.frontendUrl: FRONTEND_URL – URL of the frontend (default: http://localhost:3000/).
envExplanation.docsImportSettings: Docs import settings – used by the documentation import feature.

collaboration.title: How to Collaborate
collaboration.description: Beyou is open-source under the Apache 2.0 license. Contributions are welcome — here's how to get involved.

repos.title: Project Repositories
repos.description: The project is split across 5 repositories under the AndDev741 GitHub organization:
repos.frontend: Beyou-Frontend — Main React app (habits, goals, tasks UI)
repos.backend: Beyou-backend-spring — Spring Boot API (auth, domain logic, persistence)
repos.devEnv: Beyou-dev-env — Docker Compose orchestration for local dev

workflow.title: Contribution Workflow
workflow.step1: Fork the repository you want to contribute to
workflow.step2: Create a feature branch from main
workflow.step3: Make your changes and run tests locally
workflow.step4: Open a Pull Request to main with a clear description

code.title: Code
code.description: Fix bugs, add features, or improve performance in the backend (Java/Spring) or frontend (React/TypeScript).

docs.title: Documentation
docs.description: Improve or add architecture docs, API specs, and design documents in the arch-design repo (Markdown).

translations.title: Translations
translations.description: Help keep English and Portuguese translations complete and accurate in the JSON translation files. New languages are welcome too!

guidelines.title: Guidelines
guidelines.bilingual: Keep all user-facing text bilingual — add both en and pt translations.
guidelines.tests: Run tests before submitting a PR (npm run test / ./mvnw test).
guidelines.patterns: Follow existing code patterns and conventions in the repository.
guidelines.smallPrs: Prefer small, focused PRs over large sweeping changes.

futureIdeas.title: Future Ideas
futureIdeas.description: Ideas for future updates to the app — feature requests that we'd love to build.

plan.description: Read-only roadmap ideas for what we want to document next.
